transcript.text: Hello, living in Lafayette, V-Log, the 2nd of May, 2013.
I'm going to do my V-Logs at night inside of the bathroom now because it's better lighting and sound.
Let's see, today I had like 11 hours of sleep, got up at like 9.30.
I didn't sleep so well last night, but I felt like I wanted to sleep in.
And then I did some organizing today that I still had to do.
And then rode on the rollers and I wanted to stand up because my dick was getting numb.
But the whole thing was like not very sturdy.
So I went down to the lumber yard and bought a bunch of 2x4s and then came back and then spent a couple hours like nailing seven of them into it.
I'll do a video just showing it later.
And then gluing it with wood glue and then painting it up with stain, you know, just to make it stronger.
And then started riding it some more.
And then, oh, while I was working on my site, condensing my candle link book page and listening to...
I busted through two books today.
History of the Revolt of the Netherlands and Alexander the Great by Jacob Abbott.
And then got started maybe a third of the way into Autobiography of a Yogi.
But then the former remit came by and got us high.
And then my lungs are totally fucked.
I did it because I just wanted to be high and ride on the trainer once.
But now I won't do it again.
I won't smoke any more of that again.
I can't smoke and ride at the same time.
So then I started like watching like Tour de France videos, like 84 Tour de France.
It's cool to watch it while I'm riding the bike.
And I ended up, yes, I did in two different sections.
The next time I'll try to do it in one.
But the last one, it didn't, it's on my computer, but it didn't make it onto Strava.
And I don't know why.
It just couldn't see it.
So maybe I'll call Garmin and see if they can help me.
Even though I can't help you with Strava, but maybe I'll figure it out.
I didn't remember pushing stop, but I hit something.
I hit it.
I hit stop.
I didn't see it.
It said timer stopped.
And then it said save, and it saved.
So I don't know what else.
But anyways, I can manually input it.
So I rode 72 kilometers, which is like 44 miles.
And I'm pretty beat right now, like more than I was before.
But that was my first big ride.
And tomorrow I'm not going to ride.
I'm going to do dumbbells for the first time.
Working out for the first time tomorrow.
Yay.
And yeah, I spent like 27 bucks today because I got a $5 sandwich when it went out.
And then I got all that wood and everything, which is $22.50.
But from here on in, I'm going to have to be spending money.

And to make videos.

And then I'll be getting into the groove.
